message:
docs(release): document sub-crate publish order + publish-script (ft-fytns)

10 sub-crates extracted under ft-y0loj must publish to crates.io in a
strict topological order — leaves first, then frankenterm-core, then
mid-tier crates that depend back on it. Without this order, cargo
publish rejects path-dep resolution because the dep crate isn't on
crates.io yet at the requested version.

Adds:
  - docs/release/sub-crate-publish-order.md — the dependency graph
    (computed from each Cargo.toml's [dependencies], dev-deps stripped
    since they're omitted at publish time), the 11-step topological
    order, the path-dep gotcha, and a reproducible verification
    one-liner so future agents can confirm the graph hasn't drifted.
  - scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh — emits cargo publish commands in
    the right order. --dry-run mode prints the commands; without it,
    publishes serially with a 30s pause between levels to let the
    crates.io index propagate. Refuses to run without
    CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN unless --dry-run.

Topological order (3 levels):
  Level 0 (parallel-safe leaves): config-types, error-types,
                                  policy-types, replay-types,
                                  resource-types, telemetry-types
  Level 1: frankenterm-core
  Level 2 (mid-tier): ars, fleet, replay, tantivy

Verified: scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh --dry-run emits 11 commands in
the documented order.

Closes ft-fytns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-8ybme","title":"[scan_pipeline] process_chunk allows append-after-flush-pending growth","description":"ChunkedPipelineState::process_chunk only exposes should_flush() as an advisory signal. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:221-223 and 450-516, callers can keep appending after the active raw-data buffer has already crossed max_buffer_bytes, so a stalled or forgotten flusher grows trigger_data_buffer/uncompressed_buffer without bound.

Repro:
1. let pipeline = ScanPipeline::default();
2. let mut state = ChunkedPipelineState::new(64);
3. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'x'; 80], &mut state);
4. state.should_flush() == true
5. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'y'; 80], &mut state);
6. raw replay buffer keeps growing instead of failing fast or forcing a flush.

Expected: once flush is pending, a subsequent non-empty process_chunk call should refuse to accumulate more bytes rather than silently converting backpressure into unbounded memory growth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:00.532379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.219221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.218185Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 658b945a: scan_pipeline now fails fast when flush is pending","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-9d4a3","title":"[MEDIUM] EventBus stats overreport queued backlog after subscribers drain channel","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:655-674 and 1942-1970. EventBus::stats() trusts runtime_compat::broadcast_len() as unread queue depth, then derives oldest lag from that count. Under the shipped asupersync broadcast implementation, len() reports ring-buffer retention rather than unread messages; the existing test stats_clear_oldest_lag_when_queue_is_drained already documents this and had to weaken the assertion instead of expecting zero/None after the sole subscriber drains the channel. Repro: create EventBus::new(4), subscribe_deltas(), publish two SegmentCaptured events, recv both successfully, then call stats(); delta_queued can stay non-zero and delta_oldest_lag_ms can keep reporting stale lag even though no backlog remains. This causes operator/metrics surfaces (ipc status, metrics export) to falsely report event-bus congestion. Expected: stats() should report unread backlog/lag, or the fields/docs should be renamed to retained-ring occupancy rather than queue depth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:14:13.498770Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232646Z","close_reason":"Track unread backlog from slowest subscriber instead of retained ring occupancy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["patterns","review"]}

 {"id":"ft-9xbxe","title":"mcp_tools test helper hits asupersync vs fastmcp_rust::asupersync version conflict","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812\` cannot compile under
\`--features mcp\`:

\`\`\`
error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812:25
     |
4812 |         McpContext::new(asupersync::Cx::for_testing(), 1)
     |         --------------- ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |                         expected \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\`,
     |                         found \`asupersync::Cx\`
     |
note: there are multiple different versions of crate \`asupersync\` in
      the dependency graph
\`\`\`

\`McpContext::new\` (defined in \`fastmcp_rust\`) expects
\`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` (i.e. fastmcp_rust's own vendored copy of
asupersync's Cx type), but the test helper passes the directly-imported
\`asupersync::Cx::for_testing()\` from the workspace's pinned
asupersync crate. With version drift between fastmcp_rust's vendored
asupersync (0.2.6 per the older fastmcp_rust pin) and the workspace
asupersync (0.2.9 per /Users/jemanuel/.cargo/registry path in the
error message), the two Cx types are nominally distinct even though
their fields are identical.

## Concrete failure path

1. Run \`cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core --features mcp\`.
2. Compiler tries to build the \`#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]\` test module
   in mcp_tools.rs.
3. \`test_mcp_context()\` at line 4811-4813 fails to type-check because
   \`McpContext::new\` from fastmcp_rust expects \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` but
   the call site passes \`asupersync::Cx\` from the workspace's
   asupersync.
4. **Entire mcp_tools test compilation blocked** — none of the ~50+
   tests in the mcp_tools test module can run, including any
   regression tests filed against mcp_tools handlers.

## Reproduction

\`\`\`
CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target \\
cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core \\
  --no-default-features --features mcp 2>&1 | grep -B2 -A10 E0308
\`\`\`

## Severity rationale

- Blocks mcp_tools test verification entirely — any new mcp_tools test
  added under \`--features mcp\` cannot be verified locally.
- Real defect-finding work in the mcp surface (e.g. ft-tzwuw which
  found 3 missing timeout_secs guards) lands without test coverage
  because the test target won't compile.
- Not a runtime bug in production code — fastmcp_rust vs asupersync
  Cx structs are runtime-compatible; only the type system rejects
  them.

## Fix sketch

One of:
1. Bump the fastmcp_rust pin so its vendored asupersync matches the
   workspace asupersync 0.2.9 (preferred — single source of truth).
2. Replace the \`test_mcp_context\` helper to use
   \`fastmcp_rust::Cx::for_testing()\` (whatever the equivalent is in
   the fastmcp_rust API surface) instead of bridging through
   asupersync directly.
3. Add an explicit conversion (\`From<asupersync::Cx> for
   fastmcp_rust::Cx\`) in fastmcp_rust if the types are
   field-compatible.

(1) is the cleanest because it eliminates the duplicated dependency.
(2) is the smallest local change.

## Scope

Diagnostic only — fix likely lives in fastmcp_rust pin (Cargo.toml)
or the test helper. Not in scope for the agent that filed this bead
unless they have context on which fastmcp_rust SHA contains the
asupersync 0.2.9 alignment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989533Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-fs42k","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses event.timestamp_ms (parallel ft-dijpe miss)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027\` passes
\`event.timestamp_ms\` (caller-supplied via the public
\`OutboundEvent::with_timestamp_ms\` builder, line 284) to
\`check_and_record\` as the dedup eviction clock — the exact shape that
ft-dijpe just fixed in the inbound bridge.

The ft-dijpe defect description even claimed *"Outbound bridge doesn't
have this shape — it uses its own \`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now()\`
for all internal state."* That claim is wrong. The outbound bridge's
constructor (line 245) does use \`SystemTime::now()\` to seed
\`timestamp_ms\` by default, but:

1. The public \`with_timestamp_ms\` builder (line 284) lets any caller
   override that default to an arbitrary value.
2. \`route_event\` line 1027 then passes that caller-controlled value
   directly to \`OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record\`, which calls
   \`evict_expired(now_ms)\` and pops anything whose
   \`now_ms - inserted_at_ms > ttl_ms\`.
3. Same replay-bypass attack as ft-dijpe: a caller submitting two
   copies of the same correlation_id with the second copy's
   \`timestamp_ms = ttl_ms + 1\` triggers eviction of the first entry
   and the replay is treated as new.

## Concrete failure path

1. Internal caller (workflow handler, retry loop, test harness) builds
   an \`OutboundEvent\` and calls \`with_timestamp_ms\` to override the
   default \`SystemTime::now()\` (legitimate reason: replaying historical
   events for backfill, fixture-driven tests, or a buggy clock-advance
   helper).
2. First dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=0\`.
3. Second dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1\`.
4. \`evict_expired\` sees \`now_ms=ttl+1 > inserted_at=0+ttl\` → pops X.
5. \`check_and_record\` finds cache empty, inserts a new X, returns
   \`true\` → second dispatch routes as if new. All downstream
   side-effects fire twice (slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR
   comment, credential broker call, etc.).

Trust model is weaker than inbound's — outbound events are
internally constructed rather than attacker-supplied — but the
defense-in-depth argument applies. A buggy internal caller that
mis-sets \`timestamp_ms\` should not silently bypass dedup, and
symmetry with the inbound bridge's hardening makes the codebase
easier to reason about.

## Severity rationale

- Same shape as ft-dijpe (silent dedup bypass), one severity level
  lower because the trust model is internal-only.
- Reachable today via any internal caller misusing
  \`with_timestamp_ms\`. Future caller bugs are likelier than
  attacker reach.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-dijpe fix: in \`route_event\` line 1027, compute
\`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()\`
and pass that to \`check_and_record\` instead of \`event.timestamp_ms\`.
The \`event.timestamp_ms\` field stays useful as an analytics signal
(end-to-end source→sink latency, dispatch-history correlation) but
must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

## Regression test

\`bridge_dedup_ignores_event_timestamp_ms_for_eviction\`: dispatch X
with \`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=0\`, then dispatch X with
\`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2\`. Assert the second is
\`deduplicated=true\`. Pre-fix the second returns \`deduplicated=false\`
because evict_expired with the spoofed future-timestamp pops the live
entry.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs\` only.
The line-1080 \`let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms\` (used downstream for
\`created_at_ms\`, \`broker_checked_at_ms\`, \`dispatched_at_ms\`) is a
separate concern — those telemetry timestamps arguably should also
be SystemTime::now() but that's a wider change. This bead covers
ONLY the dedup eviction clock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:25:28.629996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.140282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.139925Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-ptjlp (same defect, same line, parallel filing race). Sibling agent already applied the SystemTime::now() fix in working tree at connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1037-1044 with their own ft-ptjlp tag in the inline comment. Closing this bead so ft-ptjlp owns the fix and the test work. Reviewed their fix — it's identical to what I would have written (SystemTime::now() → dedup_now_ms → check_and_record), correctly preserves event.timestamp_ms for downstream telemetry uses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fvt0b","title":"codec oversized-container regression overconstrains error wording","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:12.478186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.315201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.314833Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4d6c4abc; verified with codec --lib and conformance_pdu_wire","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fy44g","title":"[review][note] reviewer audit converged — 5 consecutive rounds with no substantive defects filed; work has saturated on polish","description":"Formal convergence signal at end of continuous review session. 109 commits audited across 27+ review rounds. Five consecutive reviewer ticks have produced zero substantive defect filings. All P0/P1 structural fixes have landed; remaining open beads are scope-bounded follow-ups, not new findings.

## Tick-level convergence evidence

- Round 25 (f540a5d5 shared rate limiter + ddf4eb8f drift catch + 0f744567 TOON tx): 0 beads
- Round 26 (ft-siwlu sanity + 97016fe6 + 7d6fdac8 11-of-12 deny audit): 0 beads
- Round 27 (d9d7d361 redactor extract + 1ada5ffe coord close): 0 beads
- Final rollup (107 commits): 0 beads
- This tick (65ea21d5 approval hints + a94abb0e AGENTS.md docs): 0 beads

## Recent commits are clearly polish

- Approval recovery hints (actionable strings pointing at ft watch and ft approve CODE)
- AGENTS.md module-map rows for the newly-landed redactor module and policy-denial audit stream
- Re-export-based module extraction with zero downstream breakage
- Coordination concerns resolving naturally without the predicted rebase storm

## Open bead backlog owned cleanly

- ft-eu0no HIGH: structural fix shipped f540a5d5 + ddf4eb8f; test execution tracked as ft-eu0no.1
- ft-2zbie MEDIUM: pane 4 TOON regeneration in flight, 9 placeholder goldens to close naturally
- ft-xydxz + ft-rpjud LOW: ftvi fuzz corpus-quality improvements (non-blocking)

## Next tick posture

Per user instruction the next pane 6 tick is targeted-audit only. This convergence note is the handoff signal: stop continuous watch, resume only on specific commit/bead target.

## Systemic state

Session-start vs session-end deltas already rolled up in the 'Final Reviewer Rollup' narrative. Security posture: MCP rate limiting / authorization / deny audit / read-path redaction all moved from patchy-or-absent to comprehensively gated. Correctness posture: tx compensation, mission dispatch, cancel propagation all moved from synthetic/fake to verified end-to-end.

No new concern. Filed as the formal convergence artifact per user request.  {"id":"ft-g3g1c","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml pins actions/attest to mutable @v4 tag","description":"release.yml:145 uses actions/attest@v4 — mutable major-version alias. Verified resolves to v4.1.0 today via GitHub releases API. actions/attest default mode (no sbomPath/predicate) IS SLSA provenance per src/detect.ts, so functionally correct. Concern: attestation action pinning by moving tag undermines its own supply-chain purpose. Fix: pin to 40-char SHA. Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:27.714567Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046980Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046712Z","close_reason":"Landed in eba16ce3: pinned actions/attest to immutable SHA 59d89421af93a897026c735860bf21b6eb4f7b26 (v4.1.0).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-g3z31","title":"[review][LOW] ft-ojgmo lacks regression test for pre-cancel fast-path in EventSubscriber::recv_cx","description":"fa1fbffe added two cancel-detection paths to EventSubscriber::recv_cx at crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:789-817:

1. Pre-cancel fast-path at :793:
       if cx.is_cancel_requested() { return Err(RecvError::Cancelled); }
2. Mid-flight cancel watcher at :798-806 (select race with 50ms polling).

The new regression test `event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_mid_flight_cancel` at :1573-1601 exercises the MID-FLIGHT path only — cancel is signalled 100ms AFTER recv_cx is entered. The PRE-CANCEL fast-path (cx already cancelled when recv_cx is called) is untested at the EventSubscriber layer.

Evidence: search for calls to EventSubscriber::recv_cx with a pre-cancelled cx in the test suite — none exist. The runtime_compat.rs test at :6189 tests pre-cancel on the lower-level broadcast_recv_with_cx primitive, but not the EventSubscriber wrapper that is the public API.

Why it matters: the pre-cancel shortcut is one line, but it was the difference between a hot loop of polling wakeups and immediate return. A refactor that moves the cancel check inside the select! block (losing the shortcut) would regress the behavior silently — the mid-flight test would still pass because the 50ms poll would eventually fire.

Fix: add a sibling test:

    #[test]
    fn event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_pre_cancel_immediately() {
        run_async_test(async {
            let cx = crate::cx::Cx::for_testing();
            cx.cancel_with(crate::outcome::CancelKind::User, Some("pre-cancel"));
            let bus = EventBus::new(8);
            let mut sub = bus.subscribe();
            let started = Instant::now();
            let result = sub.recv_cx(&cx).await;
            assert!(matches!(result, Err(RecvError::Cancelled)));
            assert!(started.elapsed() < Duration::from_millis(5),
                "pre-cancel must return immediately, took {:?}", started.elapsed());
        });
    }

A <5ms assertion distinguishes the shortcut path from the 50ms poll path.

Severity: LOW — not a correctness bug, just a coverage gap. Discovered during reviewer audit round 11, 2026-04-23 while verifying fa1fbffe.

Tag: review, events, cancellation, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:57.398125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.679034Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.678785Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 8ae03256: add EventSubscriber::recv_cx pre-cancel regression test that asserts immediate Err(Cancelled) before any emission.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-g43fq","title":"[ft-7iof6.1] rename runtime_compat → runtime_async with one-release deprecated re-export","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:26.173200Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:13:51.947751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:13:51.947464Z","close_reason":"0f15e1bc renames the canonical async surface module and its surface_guard sibling, with a one-release deprecated alias preserving 187 files / 1622 references. Same shape as cc7's ft-zoxxq.1 (8bf2a272) WeztermInterface→MuxInterface rename. Concrete changes: (1) git mv runtime_compat.rs → runtime_async.rs (clean rename, 100% similar), (2) git mv runtime_compat_surface_guard.rs → runtime_async_surface_guard.rs (98% similar — only the one internal RAW_TOKIO_RUNTIME_BUILDER_QUARANTINE_V1 path updated), (3) lib.rs replaces the two pub mod declarations and adds #[deprecated] pub use … as old_name aliases for both modules so callers keep compiling, (4) AGENTS.md updated in 4 places to reflect the rename has landed. Compile-time alias works: every existing crate::runtime_compat::Foo resolves through the alias to runtime_async::Foo. The #[deprecated] note doesn't propagate through pub-use module aliases (known Rust gotcha, same as cc7's WeztermInterface alias) — call-site sweep is left for a follow-up bead. cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target. Did not touch codec/ or frankenterm/client/ (cc7 lane).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-g43fq","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:31.145568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,124 @@
+# Sub-crate publish order (ft-fytns)
+
+The ft-y0loj monolith-split work has shipped 10 sub-crates carved out
+of `frankenterm-core`. They publish in a strict topological order:
+**leaves first, then `frankenterm-core`, then mid-tier sub-crates that
+depend back on it.** Skipping the order causes `cargo publish` to fail
+with "no matching package named X found" because the sub-crate's
+versioned dep on a not-yet-published crate cannot resolve from
+crates.io.
+
+The release script `scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh` emits the commands
+in the right order. This doc is the *why*.
+
+## Dependency graph
+
+Computed from each `Cargo.toml`'s `[dependencies]` (regular only;
+`[dev-dependencies]` are stripped at publish time and don't constrain
+ordering).
+
+```
+Level 0 — true leaves (no frankenterm-* deps):
+  frankenterm-core-config-types
+  frankenterm-core-error-types
+  frankenterm-core-policy-types
+  frankenterm-core-replay-types
+  frankenterm-core-resource-types
+  frankenterm-core-telemetry-types
+
+Level 1 — frankenterm-core (depends on all 6 leaves):
+  frankenterm-core
+
+Level 2 — mid-tier (depend on frankenterm-core ± leaves):
+  frankenterm-core-ars       → frankenterm-core
+  frankenterm-core-fleet     → frankenterm-core + frankenterm-core-resource-types
+  frankenterm-core-replay    → frankenterm-core + frankenterm-core-replay-types
+  frankenterm-core-tantivy   → frankenterm-core
+```
+
+`frankenterm-core` itself dev-depends on the 3 of 4 mid-tier crates
+(ars, replay, tantivy) for its integration tests. Cargo allows dev-dep
+cycles in the workspace; at publish time the `[dev-dependencies]`
+section is omitted, so the cycle does not block publishing.
+
+## Topological order
+
+```
+1. frankenterm-core-config-types
+2. frankenterm-core-error-types
+3. frankenterm-core-policy-types
+4. frankenterm-core-replay-types
+5. frankenterm-core-resource-types
+6. frankenterm-core-telemetry-types
+7. frankenterm-core
+8. frankenterm-core-ars
+9. frankenterm-core-fleet
+10. frankenterm-core-replay
+11. frankenterm-core-tantivy
+```
+
+Levels 0 and 2 are internally parallel-safe — items within the same
+level have no inter-dependencies — but for predictable failure
+reporting the script publishes serially.
+
+## Path-deps will block publishing
+
+Every sub-crate's `Cargo.toml` declares its inter-workspace deps as
+`{ path = "../frankenterm-core-X" }`. Cargo accepts this for local
+development but **rejects** it during `cargo publish` unless paired
+with a `version = "x.y.z"` field. The release sequence either:
+
+- adds explicit `version = "..."` fields next to every path dep
+  before publishing, **or**
+- relies on `cargo publish`'s implicit version-from-workspace
+  inheritance (works only when the dep crate is already on
+  crates.io at the requested version).
+
+Practically: bump every workspace-member version in lockstep, push
+them together, and run the publish script in order. Don't try to
+publish only one sub-crate at a time without first ensuring its
+dependencies are already on crates.io at the matching version.
+
+## Running the publish script
+
+```sh
+# Dry-run (prints the cargo commands but does not execute):
+scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh --dry-run
+
+# Actually publish (requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN):
+scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh
+```
+
+The script aborts on the first failure so you can re-run from the
+breakpoint after fixing whatever blocked the publish (typically a
+missing version field on a path dep, or a dirty working tree).
+
+## Adding a new sub-crate
+
+When ft-y0loj.x lands a new sub-crate:
+
+1. Add it to `scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh` in its correct
+   topological slot (see the levels above).
+2. Update the dependency graph in this doc.
+3. Verify `cargo publish --dry-run -p <new-crate>` succeeds in a
+   fresh checkout before claiming the bead complete.
+
+## Verification
+
+The script and this doc were reconciled against the workspace at
+ft-fytns close. Re-run this sanity check after any new extraction:
+
+```sh
+for c in crates/frankenterm-core-*; do
+  name=$(basename "$c")
+  deps=$(grep -E "^frankenterm-(core|alloc)" "$c/Cargo.toml" \
+    | sed -E 's/ ?=.*//' | sort -u | grep -v "^$name$" \
+    | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
+  printf "%-38s deps: %s\n" "$name" "${deps:-<none>}"
+done
+```
+
+The output should match the dependency graph above. If a dep edge
+appears that crosses a level (e.g. a leaf gaining a `frankenterm-core`
+dep), the topological order in the script needs revisiting before the
+next release.

@@ -0,0 +1,110 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# ft-fytns — publish frankenterm-core sub-crates in topological dep order.
+#
+# Why this exists:
+# `cargo publish` rejects `path = "../X"` deps unless the target crate
+# is already on crates.io at the requested version. The 10 sub-crates
+# carved out under ft-y0loj have a layered dep graph that imposes a
+# strict publish order:
+#
+#   1. 6 leaves (no frankenterm-* deps)
+#   2. frankenterm-core (depends on all 6 leaves)
+#   3. 4 mid-tier (depend on frankenterm-core ± leaves)
+#
+# See docs/release/sub-crate-publish-order.md for the full rationale and
+# the verification one-liner that confirms the graph hasn't drifted.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh --dry-run    # print commands only
+#   scripts/publish-sub-crates.sh              # actually publish
+#
+# Requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN unless --dry-run.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+DRY_RUN=false
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--dry-run" ]]; then
+    DRY_RUN=true
+fi
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
+cd "${REPO_ROOT}"
+
+# Topological order. Crates within the same level are listed in
+# alphabetical order for predictable output; they have no
+# inter-dependencies and could be published in parallel, but we serialize
+# for clearer failure reporting.
+declare -a LEVEL_0=(
+    "frankenterm-core-config-types"
+    "frankenterm-core-error-types"
+    "frankenterm-core-policy-types"
+    "frankenterm-core-replay-types"
+    "frankenterm-core-resource-types"
+    "frankenterm-core-telemetry-types"
+)
+declare -a LEVEL_1=(
+    "frankenterm-core"
+)
+declare -a LEVEL_2=(
+    "frankenterm-core-ars"
+    "frankenterm-core-fleet"
+    "frankenterm-core-replay"
+    "frankenterm-core-tantivy"
+)
+
+publish() {
+    local crate="$1"
+    local level="$2"
+    if [[ ! -d "crates/${crate}" ]]; then
+        echo "ft-fytns: missing crate dir crates/${crate}" >&2
+        echo "  the sub-crate may have been renamed or the script drifted from the workspace" >&2
+        return 2
+    fi
+    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
+        printf "[level %s] cargo publish -p %s\n" "${level}" "${crate}"
+    else
+        printf "[level %s] publishing %s ...\n" "${level}" "${crate}"
+        cargo publish -p "${crate}"
+        # Wait briefly so the index has time to propagate before the
+        # next crate's publish attempts to resolve its dep on this one.
+        # crates.io's index typically catches up within seconds, but
+        # being conservative here turns "transient resolution failure"
+        # into "deterministic success".
+        if [[ "${level}" != "2" ]]; then
+            sleep 30
+        fi
+    fi
+}
+
+main() {
+    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "false" && -z "${CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        echo "ft-fytns: CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN not set; refusing to publish" >&2
+        echo "  rerun with --dry-run to preview commands without publishing" >&2
+        return 2
+    fi
+
+    echo "ft-fytns: sub-crate publish order (10 crates across 3 dep levels)"
+    echo
+
+    for crate in "${LEVEL_0[@]}"; do
+        publish "${crate}" 0
+    done
+
+    for crate in "${LEVEL_1[@]}"; do
+        publish "${crate}" 1
+    done
+
+    for crate in "${LEVEL_2[@]}"; do
+        publish "${crate}" 2
+    done
+
+    echo
+    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
+        echo "ft-fytns: dry-run complete; run without --dry-run to actually publish."
+    else
+        echo "ft-fytns: published 10 sub-crates in topological order."
+    fi
+}
+
+main "$@"
